***Transformers: The Last Knight*** is a 2017 American [science fiction action film](/wiki/Science_fiction_action_film "Science fiction action film") based on [Hasbro](/wiki/Hasbro "Hasbro")'s *[Transformers](/wiki/Transformers_(toy_line) "Transformers (toy line)")* toy line. It is the sequel to [Transformers: Age of Extinction](/wiki/Transformers:_Age_of_Extinction "Transformers: Age of Extinction") (2014) and the fifth installment in the [*Transformers* film series](/wiki/Transformers_(film_series)#Films "Transformers (film series)"). The film was directed by [Michael Bay](/wiki/Michael_Bay "Michael Bay") and written by [Art Marcum, Matt Holloway](/wiki/Art_Marcum_and_Matt_Holloway "Art Marcum and Matt Holloway"), and [Ken Nolan](/wiki/Ken_Nolan "Ken Nolan"). It stars [Mark Wahlberg](/wiki/Mark_Wahlberg "Mark Wahlberg") reprising his role from the fourth film, while [Josh Duhamel](/wiki/Josh_Duhamel "Josh Duhamel"), [John Turturro](/wiki/John_Turturro "John Turturro") and [Glenn Morshower](/wiki/Glenn_Morshower "Glenn Morshower") reprising their roles from the first three films. [Stanley Tucci](/wiki/Stanley_Tucci "Stanley Tucci") returns as a different character after also appearing in the fourth film, with [Anthony Hopkins](/wiki/Anthony_Hopkins "Anthony Hopkins") joining the cast. In the film, inventor Cade Yeager is gifted a Talisman by a dying Transformer knight, and is soon recruited by an eccentric British professor to save the world from the impending threat of a Cybertronian Sorceress.

*Transformers: The Last Knight* premiered on June 18, 2017, at the [Odeon Luxe Leicester Square](/wiki/Odeon_Luxe_Leicester_Square "Odeon Luxe Leicester Square") in [London](/wiki/London "London") and was released in the United States on June 21, by [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures"). The film received generally negative reviews from critics, and grossed $605 million against a production budget between $217–260 million and losing Paramount an estimated $100 million on the theatrical release. It was followed by a prequel, *[Bumblebee](/wiki/Bumblebee_(film) "Bumblebee (film)")*, in 2018.

## Plot

In 484 AD, [Merlin](/wiki/Merlin "Merlin") finds the Knights of Iacon, a group of Transformers hiding on Earth, seeking their help to aid [King Arthur](/wiki/King_Arthur "King Arthur") and his [knights](/wiki/Knights_of_the_Round_Table "Knights of the Round Table"). They hand him a staff and help Arthur defeat the [Saxons](/wiki/Saxons "Saxons"), but warn Merlin to hide the staff.

In the present day, following the events of Hong Kong,[[a]](#cite_note-Timeline-7) [Optimus Prime](/wiki/Optimus_Prime "Optimus Prime") arrives on the ruins of Cybertron and meets the Cybertronian goddess [Quintessa](/wiki/Quintessons "Quintessons"), who brainwashes him into becoming Nemesis Prime and sends him to Earth to retrieve Merlin's staff, which will restore Cybertron by taking Earth's energy core.

On Earth, a new [paramilitary](/wiki/Paramilitary "Paramilitary") [task force](/wiki/Task_force "Task force") called the Transformers Reaction Force (TRF) continues to hunt Transformers, while some of its [U.S. military](/wiki/U.S._military "U.S. military") personnel, including former [NEST](/wiki/NEST_(Transformers) "NEST (Transformers)") members Colonel William Lennox and General Morshower, are reluctantly against its actions. Cade Yeager, an ally to the [Autobots](/wiki/Autobots "Autobots"), hides Transformer refugees in his junkyard. In a war-torn [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), Cade and [Bumblebee](/wiki/Bumblebee_(Transformers) "Bumblebee (Transformers)") meet a scavenger named Izabella and her Transformer companion, Sqweeks, before encountering a dying Transformer who gives them a talisman. The TRF confronts Cade, only to be stopped by Bumblebee, Lennox, and Hound before letting them go.

[Megatron](/wiki/Megatron "Megatron"), having abandoned his Galvatron identity, his remaining [Decepticons](/wiki/Decepticons "Decepticons"), and the [U.S. government](/wiki/U.S._government "U.S. government") learn of the talisman's value and reluctantly join forces to retrieve it and track Bumblebee to Cade's scrapyard. While the Autobots and the Dinobots fend off the Decepticons and the TRF, Cade, his assistant Jimmy, Izabella, and Sqweeks deal with a swarm of TRF drones sent after them. Cogman, a human-sized Transformer, appears and invites Cade to the United Kingdom to meet his employer, Sir Edmund Burton, who is connected to the Transformers. Burton asks another Transformer, Hot Rod, to find Viviane Wembly, a [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford") professor.

Burton explains to Cade, Viviane, and Bumblebee that Transformers have been living amongst mankind [for centuries](/wiki/Ancient_astronauts "Ancient astronauts"), their existence once guarded by a [secret society](/wiki/Secret_society "Secret society") called the Order of Witwiccans, of which he is the last living member. The talisman can lead to Merlin's staff, buried in a Cybertronian spacecraft under the sea. Viviane is revealed to be the direct descendant of Merlin's bloodline and the only one who can activate the staff. The authorities discover them, forcing the group to flee. Following clues, while evading their pursuers, the group heads to the [Royal Navy](/wiki/Royal_Navy "Royal Navy") Museum, where they commandeer the submarine [HMS *Alliance*](/wiki/HMS_Alliance_(P417) "HMS Alliance (P417)"), also a Transformer, to find the spacecraft while the TRF and the [Navy SEALs](/wiki/United_States_Navy_SEALs "United States Navy SEALs") tail them.

Burton contacts Seymour Simmons, both learning that Earth is [Unicron](/wiki/Unicron "Unicron"), and the staff will drain the planet's life via an access point under [Stonehenge](/wiki/Stonehenge "Stonehenge"). Locating the ship, Cade and Viviane find Merlin's tomb with Viviane activating Merlin's buried staff, awakening the Knights of Iacon. The TRF and Navy SEALs ambush Cade and Viviane to take the staff, but Viviane is forced to surrender it to Optimus, who has arrived to take the staff. Bumblebee, Cade, and Lennox engage Optimus, during which Bumblebee speaks in his voice for the first time after he is given a spare voice box, stirring Optimus's memories and freeing him from Quintessa's control. The Decepticons ambush Optimus and Cade and steal the staff, having aligned themselves with Quintessa. The Knights arrive to execute Optimus but yield to Cade and join the humans and the Autobots upon seeing Cade's talisman turn into [Excalibur](/wiki/Excalibur "Excalibur"). Burton tries to stop Megatron from activating the staff at Stonehenge, only to be killed in the ensuing blast.

As Cybertron ravages Earth, using its thermal energy to reconstitute itself, the Autobots, Dragonstorm, and the humans launch a direct assault on Cybertron to defeat the Decepticons and Quintessa. Viviane removes the staff and stops the transfer. Optimus defeats Megatron while Bumblebee seemingly kills Quintessa. With both worlds saved but now attached, the Autobots leave Earth to rebuild Cybertron.

Meanwhile, Quintessa, who has survived, disguises herself as a human and approaches a group of scientists inspecting one of Unicron's horns and offers them a way to destroy him.

## Production

### Development

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Michael.bay.png/250px-Michael.bay.png)](/wiki/File:Michael.bay.png)

[Michael Bay](/wiki/Michael_Bay "Michael Bay") announced that it would be the last *Transformers* film he would direct.

Following the release of *[Transformers: Age of Extinction](/wiki/Transformers:_Age_of_Extinction "Transformers: Age of Extinction")* (2014), [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures") tasked [Akiva Goldsman](/wiki/Akiva_Goldsman "Akiva Goldsman") in March 2015 to work with the film franchise's director [Michael Bay](/wiki/Michael_Bay "Michael Bay"), executive producer [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg"), and producer [Lorenzo di Bonaventura](/wiki/Lorenzo_di_Bonaventura "Lorenzo di Bonaventura") to set up a [writers' room](/wiki/Writers%27_room "Writers' room") to create ideas for future *[Transformers](/wiki/Transformers_(film_series) "Transformers (film series)")* films.[[50]](#cite_note-51) According to Goldsman, the writing team would look at various *Transformers* media created by [Hasbro](/wiki/Hasbro "Hasbro") for inspiration; if they found one that interested them, they could submit a treatment, which would then be further developed by the whole team.[[51]](#cite_note-Writer's_Room-52) In July 2015, Goldsman and [Jeff Pinkner](/wiki/Jeff_Pinkner "Jeff Pinkner") signed on as writers.[[52]](#cite_note-53) On November 20, due to Goldsman's commitments creating a writers' room for *[G.I. Joe](/wiki/G.I._Joe "G.I. Joe")* and *[Micronauts](/wiki/Micronauts "Micronauts")* properties, Paramount began to negotiate with [Art Marcum and Matt Holloway](/wiki/Art_Marcum_and_Matt_Holloway "Art Marcum and Matt Holloway"), as well as [Ken Nolan](/wiki/Ken_Nolan "Ken Nolan"), to write the film. Lindsey Beer and [Geneva Robertson-Dworet](/wiki/Geneva_Robertson-Dworet "Geneva Robertson-Dworet") were also brought aboard for writing duties.[[53]](#cite_note-54)

> Perhaps...*Transformers 5* and *6* movies will go back more to its roots. There was an occasion where one line [in *Transformers: Age of Extinction*] which Optimus Prime had, I did not want to say. It was my gut instinct and certainly my commitment to the character... not to say the line. But I was told to say. You can't fight the big boys. I think you all know what that line was.

— [Peter Cullen](/wiki/Peter_Cullen "Peter Cullen") on a question from a fan on the future of the franchise from [Sac-Anime 2015](/wiki/Sac-Anime "Sac-Anime")[[54]](#cite_note-55)

Bay had decided not to direct any future *Transformers* films after *Age of Extinction*, but in early January 2016, he stated that he would return to direct the fifth film as his last.[[55]](#cite_note-RS-56) [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures") spent $80 million on production in Michigan, in return for $21 million in [state incentives](/wiki/Tax_incentives "Tax incentives"), under agreements entered into before the state legislature eliminated the film office incentive program in July 2015.[[56]](#cite_note-57) In April 2016, Paramount hired cinematographer [Jonathan Sela](/wiki/Jonathan_Sela "Jonathan Sela").[[57]](#cite_note-sela-58)[[58]](#cite_note-59) On May 17, Bay revealed the official title of the film to be *The Last Knight* on his [Instagram](/wiki/Instagram "Instagram") account, where he also posted a production video showing a close-up of Optimus Prime's face with purple eyes instead of blue, and his face mostly discolored.[[59]](#cite_note-60) The official Twitter account showed a 19-second short video in morse code that translates to "I'm coming for you May 31".[[60]](#cite_note-61) On May 31, it was revealed that Megatron would return in the sequel.[[43]](#cite_note-tfw2005.com-44)

### Filming

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Alnwick_Castle_02.jpg/250px-Alnwick_Castle_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Alnwick_Castle_02.jpg)

[Alnwick Castle](/wiki/Alnwick_Castle "Alnwick Castle") in [Alnwick](/wiki/Alnwick "Alnwick"), [Northumberland](/wiki/Northumberland "Northumberland"), one of the many locations used in the film

[Principal photography](/wiki/Principal_photography "Principal photography") began on May 25, 2016, in [Havana](/wiki/Havana "Havana"), [Cuba](/wiki/Cuba "Cuba"), with a few scenes shot by a "small team".[[73]](#cite_note-May2016CS-74)[[74]](#cite_note-75)[[75]](#cite_note-76) Filming continued on June 6, 2016, in [Phoenix, Arizona](/wiki/Phoenix,_Arizona "Phoenix, Arizona"), and on June 19, 2016, in [Detroit](/wiki/Detroit "Detroit"), [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"), under the [working title](/wiki/Working_title "Working title") *E75*,[[62]](#cite_note-tfw-63)[[63]](#cite_note-VVf-64)[[76]](#cite_note-77) with additional filming taking place in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois").[[77]](#cite_note-78) In Detroit, filming took place in the [Michigan Motion Pictures Studio](/wiki/Motown_Motion_Picture_Studios "Motown Motion Picture Studios"), [Packard Plant](/wiki/Packard_Automotive_Plant "Packard Automotive Plant"), [Michigan Central Station](/wiki/Michigan_Central_Station "Michigan Central Station"), Cafe D'Mongo's Speakeasy, and [MGM Grand Detroit](/wiki/MGM_Grand_Detroit "MGM Grand Detroit").[[78]](#cite_note-79) Production moved to [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") on August 21, and filming commenced on August 22 in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") and [Wales](/wiki/Wales "Wales"). In [England](/wiki/England "England"), filming took place in [North Yorkshire](/wiki/North_Yorkshire "North Yorkshire"), [Newcastle upon Tyne](/wiki/Newcastle_upon_Tyne "Newcastle upon Tyne"), [Northumberland](/wiki/Northumberland "Northumberland"), [London](/wiki/London "London"), [Gosport](/wiki/Gosport "Gosport") and [Stonehenge](/wiki/Stonehenge "Stonehenge"). Between August and October, filming occurred in [Northern Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"), and [Preikestolen](/wiki/Preikestolen "Preikestolen"), [Trolltunga](/wiki/Trolltunga "Trolltunga") and [Atlanterhavsveien](/wiki/Atlanterhavsveien "Atlanterhavsveien") in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway").[[79]](#cite_note-TransformersEuropeFilming-80)[[80]](#cite_note-81) The Trinity Library Oxford scene was filmed in [the Old Library, Trinity College Dublin](/wiki/Library_of_Trinity_College_Dublin "Library of Trinity College Dublin").[[81]](#cite_note-82)

In early September 2016, filming took place in [Alnwick Castle](/wiki/Alnwick_Castle "Alnwick Castle") in [Alnwick](/wiki/Alnwick "Alnwick") [Northumberland](/wiki/Northumberland "Northumberland") in England, including car chase scenes.[[82]](#cite_note-83) Production continued at St Aidan's Church, Seahouses, where Wahlberg spent an hour inside the church and reportedly donated £200. Rev Father Des McGiven said: "I didn't even know he was in church until he left. One of my parishioners, Danielle Love, recognized him and explained who he was. It's great that we had him in for the service, and we appreciate his generosity towards our church." Car chases were also filmed at the Monument area of Newcastle upon Tyne, where Josh Duhamel was filming, while Wahlberg and Sir Anthony Hopkins continued at Alnwick, Seahouses and the Bamburgh Region of Northumberland.[[83]](#cite_note-84) In late September 2016, filming was spotted in [Gosport](/wiki/Gosport "Gosport"), [Hampshire](/wiki/Hampshire "Hampshire") at the Royal Navy Submarine Museum. On October 5, 2016, filming was spotted at [St Bartholomew-the-Great](/wiki/St_Bartholomew-the-Great "St Bartholomew-the-Great"), London. London filming wrapped on October 27, 2016.[[84]](#cite_note-85) Principal photography wrapped on December 4, 2016.[[85]](#cite_note-Entertainment-Weekly-86)

Scenes were shot in at least four different [aspect ratios](/wiki/Aspect_ratio_(image) "Aspect ratio (image)"), including 1.90:1 ([IMAX](/wiki/IMAX "IMAX")), 2.00:1 ([Univisium](/wiki/Univisium "Univisium")), 2.28:1, & 2.39:1 ([Panavision](/wiki/Panavision "Panavision")). This has been stated by the movie's director of photography, Jonathan Sela, to be due to a large number of different types of cameras on set, including the Red 6K Weapon Dragon, the Alexa IMAX 3-D rig and the IMAX 3-D Phantom 65.

### Effects

As with previous *Transformers* installments, [Industrial Light & Magic](/wiki/Industrial_Light_%26_Magic "Industrial Light & Magic") served as the main visual effects company for *Transformers: The Last Knight*. In early 2016, the company showed Bay an underwater rendering of a crash-landed alien spaceship and a new dump-truck Transformer with a cloak.[[55]](#cite_note-RS-56)

### Music

Main article: [Transformers: The Last Knight (soundtrack)](/wiki/Transformers:_The_Last_Knight_(soundtrack) "Transformers: The Last Knight (soundtrack)")

On September 27, it was confirmed that [Steve Jablonsky](/wiki/Steve_Jablonsky "Steve Jablonsky") would return to write the score, having composed the music for the first four films. The score was released digitally on the film's original release date, Friday June 23, 2017, and a limited-edition two-disc CD set of 3,000 units were released by La-La Land Records on July 25, 2017. Unlike the previous films' scores, which contained anywhere from fourteen to twenty-three tracks, the film's soundtrack contains thirty-four tracks, amounting to over two hours of music.

On the score, Jablonsky said, "I met with Michael before he started shooting 'Transformers: The Last Knight.' He showed me some amazing concept art and explained how the story connects the history of Transformers all the way back to the times of King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table. I loved the idea because it gave me the opportunity to explore new musical ideas. The storyline allowed me to write melodies that are a bit more 'classical' than I've written for the other Transformers films, which was a lot of fun for me. Another important aspect of the story revolves around massive skyscraper-sized 'horns from hell' that start emerging from the Earth. They look like gigantic animal horns, but no one knows what they are or why they have appeared. I wanted to create an unsettling sense of mystery and tension with the music."[[86]](#cite_note-87)

### Controversy

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Blenheim_Palace_cropped.jpg/250px-Blenheim_Palace_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Blenheim_Palace_cropped.jpg)

[Blenheim Palace](/wiki/Blenheim_Palace "Blenheim Palace"), where Britain's former wartime Prime Minister Sir [Winston Churchill](/wiki/Winston_Churchill "Winston Churchill") was born, was draped in huge [Nazi flags](/wiki/Nazi_symbolism "Nazi symbolism").

On September 21, 2016, shooting for a particular scene took place in [Blenheim Palace](/wiki/Blenheim_Palace "Blenheim Palace"), [Woodstock, Oxfordshire](/wiki/Woodstock,_Oxfordshire "Woodstock, Oxfordshire") in [England](/wiki/England "England"), the home of Sir [Winston Churchill](/wiki/Winston_Churchill "Winston Churchill"), the British Prime Minister during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). The mansion was dressed as a [Nazi swastika-draped](/wiki/Nazi_symbolism "Nazi symbolism") headquarters for [Adolf Hitler](/wiki/Adolf_Hitler "Adolf Hitler") for filming. This evoked both anger and criticism for being "symbolically disrespectful to Churchill", according to Colonel [Richard Kemp](/wiki/Richard_Kemp "Richard Kemp"), who said Churchill "will be turning in his grave", while Tony Hayes of the UK Veterans Association stated that surviving World War II ex-servicemen and women would be "appalled".[[87]](#cite_note-TGNazi-88) Churchill's grandson and British Parliament member Sir [Nicholas Soames](/wiki/Nicholas_Soames "Nicholas Soames") dismissed the controversy entirely, stating, "They've no idea what my grandfather would have thought!"[[88]](#cite_note-89) Churchill is buried less than a mile away, at [St Martin's Church, Bladon](/wiki/St_Martin%27s_Church,_Bladon "St Martin's Church, Bladon").[[89]](#cite_note-THRNazi-90)

## Release

### Theatrical

*Transformers: The Last Knight* was released on June 21, 2017, after being moved up from its original June 23 release date.[[90]](#cite_note-Release-91)[[91]](#cite_note-92)[[92]](#cite_note-93)

### Home media

*Transformers: The Last Knight* was released on Digital HD on September 12, 2017, and on Blu-ray, Blu-ray 4K, Blu-ray 3D and DVD on September 26, 2017, in North America.[[93]](#cite_note-94)[[94]](#cite_note-95) It was also released in a collection with the four previous films. The film grossed $36.3 million in home sales.[[95]](#cite_note-96)

## Reception

### Box office

*Transformers: The Last Knight* grossed $130.1 million in the United States and Canada and $475.3 million in other territories for a worldwide total of $605.4 million, against a production budget of $217–260 million.[[6]](#cite_note-BOM-6)[[5]](#cite_note-2017FFS-5)
The film reportedly lost Paramount over $100 million and was deemed a commercial failure due to competition from *[Despicable Me 3](/wiki/Despicable_Me_3 "Despicable Me 3")*, *[Wonder Woman](/wiki/Wonder_Woman_(2017_film) "Wonder Woman (2017 film)")*, *[Spider-Man: Homecoming](/wiki/Spider-Man:_Homecoming "Spider-Man: Homecoming")*, *[War for the Planet of the Apes](/wiki/War_for_the_Planet_of_the_Apes "War for the Planet of the Apes")*, and *[Dunkirk](/wiki/Dunkirk_(2017_film) "Dunkirk (2017 film)")* with the latter making the least in worldwide earnings when comparing the five films but *Transformers: The Last Knight* had the least total in North America.[[96]](#cite_note-CEO-97)[[97]](#cite_note-98)

In North America, *The Last Knight* was originally projected to gross $70–75 million from 4,069 theaters over its first five days, which would have been the lowest debut of the franchise.[[98]](#cite_note-99) However, after the film grossed a franchise-low $15.7 million on its first day (including $5.5 million from Tuesday night previews), opening estimates were lowered to $60–65 million. On Thursday, it grossed $8.1 million, potentially dropping the five-day debut to under $60 million.[[99]](#cite_note-opening-100) It ended up having an opening weekend of $44.7 million, the lowest debut of the franchise by $25 million (and lowest since the first film's $70.5 million). The film's five-day gross of $68.5 million was also lower than every three-day opening of the previous four films.[[100]](#cite_note-opening2-101) The film grossed $16.9 million in its second weekend, dropping 62.2% and finishing third at the box office,[[101]](#cite_note-open2-102) and $6.4 million in its third, dropping an identical 62.2% and finishing 5th.[[102]](#cite_note-103)

Internationally, *The Last Knight* opened in its first 42 international territories alongside its United States debut, including major markets China, the UK, Russia, Australia, Germany, Italy, South Korea, and Hong Kong, and was projected to have an opening of $167–200 million.[[103]](#cite_note-budget-104)[[104]](#cite_note-DeadOpen-105) Due to its predecessor's success in China, expectations were high for the film in the country. Box office observers and trackers believed the film would open to $80–100 million,[[104]](#cite_note-DeadOpen-105) and end its run there with anywhere between $290[[105]](#cite_note-106) to $400 million.[[106]](#cite_note-107) The film ended up having a global debut of $265.3 million ($196.2 million from other territories), including $123.4 million from China, representing 63% of the film's international opening. In the film's second week in China, it fell 76% and had a running cumulative total of $147.6 million.[[100]](#cite_note-opening2-101) The film's largest markets outside North America were: China ($228.8 million), South Korea ($19.2 million), Russia ($15.9 million), Germany ($15.4 million), Mexico ($15.3 million), U.K. ($12.2 million), Brazil ($13.7 million), Indonesia ($11.7 million), Japan ($15.7 million) and France ($11 million).[[107]](#cite_note-108)

### Critical response

On [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), *Transformers: The Last Knight* has an approval rating of 16% based on 254 reviews and an average rating of 3.5/10, the lowest of the seven films in the *Transformers* series. The site's critical consensus reads, "Cacophonous, thinly plotted, and boasting state-of-the-art special effects, *The Last Knight* is pretty much what you'd expect from the fifth installment of the *Transformers* franchise."[[108]](#cite_note-rt-109) On [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), the film has a [weighted average](/wiki/Weighted_average "Weighted average") score of 27 out of 100 based on 47 critics, indicating "generally unfavorable reviews".[[109]](#cite_note-110) Audiences polled by [CinemaScore](/wiki/CinemaScore "CinemaScore") gave the film an average grade of "B+" on an A+ to F scale, the same as *[Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen](/wiki/Transformers:_Revenge_of_the_Fallen "Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen")* (2009),[[99]](#cite_note-opening-100) while [PostTrak](/wiki/PostTrak "PostTrak") reported filmgoers gave a 75% overall positive score and a 55% "definite recommend".[[100]](#cite_note-opening2-101)

[Peter Travers](/wiki/Peter_Travers "Peter Travers") of *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* gave the film a zero-star review, as he did to the previous films, saying "Every time Michael Bay directs another *Transformers* abomination (this is the fifth), the movies die a little. This one makes the summer's other blockbuster misfires look like masterpieces."[[110]](#cite_note-111) Mike Ryan of [Uproxx](/wiki/Uproxx "Uproxx") gave the film a negative review, criticizing its running time and incoherent plot, writing: "I have no proof *Transformers: The Last Knight* will kill your brain cells, but I wouldn't be surprised to learn that it does and I'd proceed with caution just in case. But I can say with absolute certainty that after watching, your head *will* hurt."[[111]](#cite_note-112) [Emily Yoshida](/wiki/Emily_Yoshida "Emily Yoshida") of [Vulture.com](/wiki/Vulture.com "Vulture.com") gave the film a negative review but wrote: "I feel slightly worried about how little I hated it." Yoshida thought it was marginally better than the previous film: "The Last Knight remains barely coherent. But it's more fun than "Age of Extinction", though both movies are so drunk on money and effects they accidentally go weird." Yoshida grudgingly praised the "visual and sonic imagination" shown by the filmmakers.[[112]](#cite_note-113) [Alonso Duralde](/wiki/Alonso_Duralde "Alonso Duralde") of *[TheWrap](/wiki/TheWrap "TheWrap")* was also critical of the film's sloppiness, saying, "...fear not, fans of the franchise: if you're here for the director's trademark chaos editing (where fights go from points A to D to Q), comedy scenes rendered tragic (and vice versa), and general full-volume confusion, you'll get all those things in abundance."[[113]](#cite_note-114)

[Peter Bradshaw](/wiki/Peter_Bradshaw "Peter Bradshaw") of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* gave it 1 out of 5, complained about the long runtime, and suggested the film was "competing with Marvel movies for spectacle" but without the "wit and fun".[[114]](#cite_note-Bradshaw-115) [Christopher Orr](/wiki/Christopher_Orr_(film_critic) "Christopher Orr (film critic)") of *[The Atlantic](/wiki/The_Atlantic "The Atlantic")* criticized the film for attempting to connect the Transformers to [Stonehenge](/wiki/Stonehenge "Stonehenge") and [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), and called it the worst retelling of Arthurian legend of the year, even worse than Guy Ritchie's *[King Arthur: Legend of the Sword](/wiki/King_Arthur:_Legend_of_the_Sword "King Arthur: Legend of the Sword")*.[[115]](#cite_note-116) Rebecca Farley of *[Refinery29](/wiki/Refinery29 "Refinery29")* and Dana Schwartz of *[Marie Claire](/wiki/Marie_Claire "Marie Claire")* were critical of the underdeveloped female characters. Farley noted the marketing made Moner appear to have a prominent role in the film, when she does not. Farley also labeled Haddock's character the film's "textbook Strong Woman" cliché. Schwartz criticizes Wahlberg's character for referring to Moner's as "Little [J. Lo](/wiki/Jennifer_Lopez "Jennifer Lopez")" because of her Latina heritage, and Haddock being portrayed as the "British [Megan Fox](/wiki/Megan_Fox "Megan Fox")". Schwartz also criticized the film for the frequent aspect ratio changes, and the runtime being too long.[[116]](#cite_note-117)[[117]](#cite_note-marieclaire-118) [Ian Freer](/wiki/Ian_Freer "Ian Freer") of *[Empire](/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)")* magazine rated the film 2 out of 5 stars. Freer said that like the previous films it "is bogged down in backstory, lacks a real feel for its characters and still can't find a way to make its robot-on-robot action exhilarating... It is amazing how a series with so much nostalgic goodwill, technical finesse and behind the scenes talent have led so often to experiences that are so joyless."[[118]](#cite_note-119)

*[*Variety*](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")'s* [Owen Gleiberman](/wiki/Owen_Gleiberman "Owen Gleiberman") gave the film a mixed review stating, "The fifth time may not quite be the charm, but the latest entry in Michael Bay's crunched-metal robot-war mega-series is badder, and therefore better."[[119]](#cite_note-120) [Richard Brody](/wiki/Richard_Brody "Richard Brody") of *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* acknowledged the film's flaws but noted there was almost something impressive about them, saying: "The absolute tastelessness of Bay's images, their stultifying service to platitudes and to merchandise, doesn't at all diminish their wildly imaginative power."[[120]](#cite_note-121) [Robbie Collin](/wiki/Robbie_Collin "Robbie Collin") of *[The Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* wrote "If you're not staggered by the technique on display here – the stuff that sets Bay's work miles above the *Fast & Furiouses*, *X-Men: Apocalypses* and Tom Cruise-chasing *Mummies* of this world – you're not paying attention" and called it "a cinematic experience of earth-shattering preposterousness".[[121]](#cite_note-122) [Justin Chang](/wiki/Justin_Chang "Justin Chang") of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* wrote: "Bay's visual sensibility has, if anything, matured, to the point of demanding and earning your exasperated surrender."[[122]](#cite_note-123) Joshua Rothkopf of *Time Out New York* gave it 3 out of 5, calling it "Clangorous and nonsensical" but "Regardless of our opinions, we all know what a Michael Bay film is. This one's his most Baysome."[[123]](#cite_note-124)

### Accolades

*Transformers: The Last Knight* led the [38th Golden Raspberry Awards](/wiki/38th_Golden_Raspberry_Awards "38th Golden Raspberry Awards") season with 10 nominations (including The Razzie Nominee So Rotten You Loved It, [Worst Picture](/wiki/Golden_Raspberry_Award_for_Worst_Picture "Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Picture"), [Worst Director](/wiki/Golden_Raspberry_Award_for_Worst_Director "Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Director"), [Worst Screen Combo](/wiki/Golden_Raspberry_Award_for_Worst_Screen_Combo "Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Screen Combo"), and [Worst Screenplay](/wiki/Golden_Raspberry_Award_for_Worst_Screenplay "Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Screenplay")) but did not win in any category, setting the record for most Razzie nominations without a win.[[124]](#cite_note-125) It was nominated for [Choice Action Movie](/wiki/Teen_Choice_Award_for_Choice_Movie_%E2%80%93_Action "Teen Choice Award for Choice Movie – Action"), Choice Summer Movie, Choice Summer Movie Actor, and Choice Summer Movie Actress at the [2017 Teen Choice Awards](/wiki/2017_Teen_Choice_Awards "2017 Teen Choice Awards").[[125]](#cite_note-TeenChoiceAwards-126) The film received nominations for Best Summer 2017 Blockbuster Trailer, Best Summer 2017 Blockbuster TV Spot (for a Feature Film), and Best TrailerByte for a Feature Film at the 2017 [Golden Trailer Awards](/wiki/Golden_Trailer_Awards "Golden Trailer Awards").[[126]](#cite_note-127)[[127]](#cite_note-128) At the [Golden Reel Awards](/wiki/Golden_Reel_Award_(Motion_Picture_Sound_Editors) "Golden Reel Award (Motion Picture Sound Editors)") 2017, *The Last Knight* was nominated for [Outstanding Achievement in Sound Editing – Feature Underscore](/wiki/Golden_Reel_Award_for_Outstanding_Achievement_in_Sound_Editing_%E2%80%93_Feature_Underscore "Golden Reel Award for Outstanding Achievement in Sound Editing – Feature Underscore").[[128]](#cite_note-129)

## Future

### Sequel

Akiva Goldsman was tasked with developing a multi-part sequel storyline, prequels, and spin-off films. Andrew Barrer and Gabriel Ferrari were hired to co-write a film that would explore the origins of Cybertron, with a working title of *Transformers One*.[[129]](#cite_note-Transformers_One_Deadline-130) Before the release of *The Last Knight*, Michael Bay said it would be his last film as director in the franchise but expressed interest in remaining as a producer to continue the series with another director. Following the critical and commercial failure of *The Last Knight*, the series' future was uncertain. By August 2017, Akiva Goldsman revealed he was no longer leading the writer's room and had left the franchise.[[130]](#cite_note-131)[[131]](#cite_note-132) Producer Lorenzo Di Bonaventura later said that the follow-up to *The Last Knight* would not be a sequel.[[132]](#cite_note-133) In an interview in September 2021, when asked about the possibility of a sequel to *The Last Knight*, Josh Duhamel had expressed interest in reprising his role as Colonel William Lennox.[[133]](#cite_note-134) In June 2025, it was announced that Bay was attached to direct a new *Transformers* film based on an idea he had pitched to the studio, with Jordan VanDina writing the screenplay.[[134]](#cite_note-135)

### Spin-offs

#### *Bumblebee* (2018)

Main article: [*Bumblebee* (film)](/wiki/Bumblebee_(film) "Bumblebee (film)")

A prequel to the film series titled *[Bumblebee](/wiki/Bumblebee_(film) "Bumblebee (film)")*, starring [Hailee Steinfeld](/wiki/Hailee_Steinfeld "Hailee Steinfeld") and [John Cena](/wiki/John_Cena "John Cena"), was released on December 21, 2018, to a successful critical and financial reception.[[135]](#cite_note-136)

In December 2018, producer Lorenzo Di Bonaventura announced that there will be further films in the series following the release of *Bumblebee*, acknowledging that the franchise will make some changes in their tone and style after the success of *Bumblebee*.[[136]](#cite_note-137)

#### *Transformers: Rise of the Beasts* (2023)

Main article: [*Transformers: Rise of the Beasts*](/wiki/Transformers:_Rise_of_the_Beasts "Transformers: Rise of the Beasts")

A sequel to *Bumblebee* titled *[Transformers: Rise of the Beasts](/wiki/Transformers:_Rise_of_the_Beasts "Transformers: Rise of the Beasts")*, starring [Anthony Ramos](/wiki/Anthony_Ramos_(actor) "Anthony Ramos (actor)") and [Dominique Fishback](/wiki/Dominique_Fishback "Dominique Fishback"), was released on June 9, 2023.[[137]](#cite_note-138)

By January 2020, a follow-up to *Bumblebee* and an adaptation of *[Transformers: Beast Wars](/wiki/Transformers:_Beast_Wars "Transformers: Beast Wars")* were in development, written separately by [Joby Harold](/wiki/Joby_Harold "Joby Harold") and [James Vanderbilt](/wiki/James_Vanderbilt "James Vanderbilt"), respectively.[[138]](#cite_note-deadline-new2022-139)[[139]](#cite_note-140) The title, *Transformers: Rise of the Beasts*, was announced with plans for it to include parts of both storylines.
